channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Infosecurity Europe

item.title: [Virtual Event] Anatomy of a Data Breach: What to Do if it Happens to You
item.description: No summary available

item.title: Anthropic’s restricted Claude Mythos model may be coming to Claude Code
item.description: Anthropic appears to be preparing for the public rollout of the Mythos model, which was announced in April as a restricted model that poses major security risks to private and public software. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Linux Flaws, Defender 0-Days, Router Botnets, and Supply Chain Chaos
item.description:
Monday recap. Same mess, new week.
A sketchy dev tool got people pwned, old bugs came back from the dead, and security products somehow needed protecting from themselves. A bunch of companies spent t...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
item.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and ...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: FBI warns of Kali365 phishing service targeting Microsoft 365 accounts
item.description: The FBI is warning about the Kali365 phishing-as-a-service platform (PhaaS) that is used to hijack Microsoft 365 accounts by abusing OAuth device code authentication to steal session tokens and bypass...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Ghost CMS CVE-2026-26980 Exploited to Hijack 700+ Sites for ClickFix Attacks
item.description:
Threat actors are exploiting a recently disclosed critical security flaw in Ghost CMS to inject malicious JavaScript code with an aim to fuel ClickFix attacks.
According to QiAnXin XLab, the activity...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The Alert Firehose Finally Meets Its Match
item.description: Ask a cybersecurity pro about Network Detection and Response (NDR) and you might still hear "Noisy," "Too much data." But ask the teams running NDR that includes agentic AI capabilities and you'll hea...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Lazarus Deploys RemotePE Memory-Only RAT Against Financial and Crypto Firms
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have shed light on a cross-platform malware called RemotePE that has been put to use by the North Korea-linked Lazarus Group in attacks targeting financial and cryptocurrency...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: TrapDoor Supply Chain Attack Spreads Credential-Stealing Malware via npm, PyPI, and CratesIO
item.description:
A new coordinated cross-ecosystem software supply chain attack campaign has targeted npm, PyPI, and Crates.io to distribute credential-stealing malware.
The campaign, codenamed TrapDoor, spans more t...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ghost CMS SQL injection flaw exploited in large-scale ClickFix campaign
item.description: A large-scale campaign is exploiting a critical SQL injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-26980) in Ghost CMS to inject malicious JavaScript code that triggers ClickFix attack flows. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Laravel Lang packages hijacked to deploy credential-stealing malware
item.description: A supply chain attack targeting the Laravel Lang localization packages has exposed developers to a sophisticated credential-stealing malware campaign after attackers abused GitHub version tags to dist...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: npm Adds 2FA-Gated Publishing and Package Install Controls Against Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: GitHub has rolled out new controls for npm to improve the security of the software supply chain, giving maintainers the ability to explicitly approve a release prior to the packages becoming publicly ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Packagist Supply Chain Attack Infects 8 Packages Using GitHub-Hosted Linux Malware
item.description:
A new "coordinated" supply chain attack campaign has impacted eight packages on Packagist including malicious code designed to run a Linux binary retrieved from a GitHub Releases URL.
"Although the a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Italy disrupts CINEMAGOAL piracy app that stole streaming auth codes
item.description: Italian authorities have dismantled a piracy ecosystem centered around the CINEMAGOAL app that provided access to various streaming platforms, including Netflix, Disney+, and Spotify. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Claude Mythos AI Finds 10,000 High-Severity Flaws in Widely Used Software
item.description: Anthropic on Friday disclosed that Project Glasswing has helped uncover more than 10,000 high- or critical-severity vulnerabilities across some of the most "systemically" important software across the...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Laravel-Lang PHP Packages Compromised to Deliver Cross-Platform Credential Stealer
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a fresh software supply chain attack campaign that has targeted multiple PHP packages belonging to Laravel-Lang to deliver a comprehensive credential-stealing f...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin CVE-2026-48172 Exploited to Run Scripts as Root
item.description:
A maximum-severity security vulnerability impacting LiteSpeed User-End cPanel Plugin has come under active exploitation in the wild.
The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-48172 (CVSS score: 10.0), relates to...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Drupal Core SQL Injection Bug Actively Exploited, Added to CISA KEV
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a recently patched critical security flaw impacting Drupal Core to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based o...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: First VPN Dismantled in Global Takedown Over Use by 25 Ransomware Groups
item.description: Authorities in Europe and North America have announced the dismantling of a criminal virtual private network (VPN) service used by criminal actors to obscure the origins of ransomware attacks, data th...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Netherlands seizes 800 servers of hosting firm enabling cyberattacks
item.description: Financial crime investigators in the Netherlands (FIOD) arrested two men and seized 800 servers linked to a web hosting company that enabled cyberattacks, interference operations, and disinformation c...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
item.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intention...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: Ghostwriter Targets Ukraine Government Entities with Prometheus Phishing Malware
item.description: The Belarus-aligned threat actor known as Ghostwriter (aka UAC-0057 and UNC1151) has been observed using lures related to Prometheus, a Ukrainian online learning platform, to target government organiz...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Akamai Joins Growing Chorus of Vendors Betting Big on Secure Enterprise Browsers
item.description: When Akamai announced its LayerX acquisition, the company joined a growing list of vendors adding secure enterprise browsers to their product portfolios....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Former US execs plead guilty to aiding tech support scammers
item.description: Two former executives of a call-tracking and analytics company pleaded guilty to concealing a years-long tech support fraud scheme that victimized individuals worldwide. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Trend Micro warns of Apex One zero-day exploited in the wild
item.description: Japanese cybersecurity software company Trend Micro has addressed an Apex One zero-day vulnerability exploited in attacks targeting Windows systems. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Verizon DBIR: Healthcare Fends Off Increased Social Engineering Attacks
item.description: Ransomware and vendor breaches persist. The "2026 Data Breach Investigations Report" (DBIR) highlights how evolving social engineering tactics make the sector more vulnerable....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Drupal: Critical SQL injection flaw now targeted in attacks
item.description: Drupal is warning that hackers are attempting to exploit a "highly critical" SQL injection vulnerability announced earlier this week. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Why Chargebacks are Just One Piece of the Fraud Puzzle
item.description: Fraud losses don't stop at chargebacks. False declines, account takeovers, and abuse also damage revenue and trust. IPQS breaks down why fraud teams need broader visibility into risk and customer impa...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Ubiquiti patches three max severity UniFi OS vulnerabilities
item.description: Ubiquiti has released security updates to patch three maximum severity vulnerabilities in UniFi OS that can be exploited by remote attackers without privileges. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
